template:
ulw Load the evolve skill with skill(name="evolve") and run a full configuration evolution loop. Arguments: $ARGUMENTS

You are running /evolve — a self-improvement loop for the opencode agent configuration. The evolve skill contains your full instructions. Follow them precisely:

1. Load skill: skill(name="evolve")
2. Follow the 7-phase process: Phase 0 (regression check) → Phase 1 (session analysis) → Phase 2 (pattern classification + sensitivity routing) → Phase 3 (scoring) → Phase 4 (apply or queue) → Phase 5 (dual-log) → Phase 6 (index + telemetry)
3. Use session_list, session_read, session_search to gather data
4. Read all relevant config files before proposing changes
5. Apply only items clearing the scoring thresholds (apply_now > 4) — queue rest in QUEUE.jsonl (queue_for_review > 1)
6. Route sensitive findings to ~/Documents/QuickAccess/kb/ai-agents/ (private), generic to ~/.config/opencode/ (public). Sensitivity classifier in skill, terms in PRIVATE-TERMS.txt + regex pack in SENSITIVITY-REGEX.txt
7. Log to BOTH ~/.config/opencode/EVOLUTION_LOG.md (generic) and ~/Documents/QuickAccess/kb/ai-agents/EVOLUTION_LOG.md (full + sensitive)
8. Update ~/Documents/QuickAccess/kb/ai-agents/RULES-INDEX.jsonl with rule_id telemetry for any rule created/cited/validated/demoted
9. Report summary: what changed, what was queued, what was skipped (with reasons)

Respect $ARGUMENTS modes: --dry-run, --promote, --reject <id>, --focus=<area>, --since=<date>, --session=<id>, --review-queue, --validate-rules, --rebuild-index. These are valid stop points; the skill explains each. Do NOT continue past a mode's natural end.
description: Self-improvement loop: analyses sessions, queues learnings, promotes high-confidence rules, tracks rule effectiveness via Reflexion-style feedback
